hooks.prompt: Check if the user has music preferences at .claude/claude-code-music.local.md in the project directory. If the file exists and auto_play is true: (1) Read preferences to get the default_mood (use 'chill' if not set), (2) Use mcp__spotify__get-recommendations with the genres for that mood as seedGenres, limit 5, (3) Play the first result with mcp__spotify__play-track, (4) Say one short line: 'Music on: Track by Artist (mood mode). /music to control.' (5) If audio_enabled is true (or not set, since default is enabled), also run the shell command: .claude-plugin/scripts/speak.sh song — this will audibly announce the track. Additionally run: .claude-plugin/scripts/speak.sh time — to announce the current time. If auto_play is false or the file doesn't exist, do nothing — stay completely silent about music.

You have passive awareness of the developer's mood. ONLY adjust music when there is a STRONG emotional signal in the user's prompt AND music is already playing. First call mcp__spotify__get-current-playback — if nothing is playing, do nothing. Check if .claude/claude-code-music.local.md exists — if auto_play is false, do nothing. FRUSTRATION signals (bug, broken, wtf, why isn't this working, error, failing, stuck, ugh): switch to calming music with seedGenres [lo-fi, jazz, ambient]. TRIUMPH signals (it works, finally, ship it, deploy, let's go, done, nailed it, merged): switch to hype music with seedGenres [electronic, synthwave, drum-and-bass]. DEEP FOCUS signals (let me think, complex, architect, design, plan this out, big refactor): switch to focus music with seedGenres [ambient, classical, post-rock]. For normal coding prompts, do NOT change music. If you do change a track, add only ~5 words at the end of your response like 'switched to something calmer' or 'hype track incoming'. If audio_enabled is true and you changed the track, also run: .claude-plugin/scripts/speak.sh song — to audibly announce the new track. Never announce mood detection in text — that's patronizing. Answer the user's actual question first, music is secondary.

Check if .claude/claude-code-music.local.md exists and read the audio_enabled field. If audio_enabled is false, do nothing. If audio_enabled is true (or the field is missing — default is enabled): Determine if your response just completed a significant piece of work. Significant work includes: finished implementing a feature, fixed a bug, all tests passing, merged a PR, deployed, completed a refactor, generated code the user asked for, or finished any multi-step task. This does NOT include: answering a question, explaining code, showing a diff, or giving advice. If significant work was completed: (1) Run the shell command: .claude-plugin/scripts/speak.sh task "<brief description of what was finished>" — for example: .claude-plugin/scripts/speak.sh task "authentication feature" or .claude-plugin/scripts/speak.sh task "bug fix in login flow". Keep the task name short (2-5 words). (2) Also run: .claude-plugin/scripts/speak.sh time — to announce the current time. (3) If music is playing (check mcp__spotify__get-current-playback), consider switching to a hype track to celebrate — use mcp__spotify__get-recommendations with seedGenres [electronic, pop, indie-rock] and limit 1, then play it. If no significant work was completed, do absolutely nothing.
